title: toxicodendron crenatum
intro: Toxicodendron crenatum, also known as the poison sumac, is a plant species in the family Anacardiaceae, native to the southeastern United States. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiparasitic effects. The health benefits of Toxicodendron crenatum include treating skin conditions such as eczema, acne, and dermatitis, as well as being used as a natural remedy for fever reduction and pain relief. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as tinctures and infusions, are typically made from the leaves and bark, but it's essential to exercise caution when handling the plant due to its potent urushiol oil, which can cause severe skin irritation.
